description: Standard: leaked internal memo from a tech company

input.document_type: Internal memo from VP of Product to executive team
input.story_context: Investigating whether a social media company knowingly allowed harmful content to remain on its platform to boost engagement
input.document_summary: A 12-page internal memo dated March 2024, from the VP of Product to the C-suite, acknowledging that the recommendation algorithm promotes sensationalist content at 3x the rate of factual content. The memo proposes a retuning of the algorithm that would reduce engagement metrics by an estimated 8% but improve content quality scores. The memo notes that the Trust & Safety team recommended this change 18 months earlier. A margin note reads 'Hold until after Q3 earnings.' The proposal was never implemented.

A one-page consulting agreement between the city and a private firm for 'strategic communications services' at $15,000 per month, signed by the deputy mayor. No scope of work defined. Term: 12 months, automatically renewing.

input.story_context: Investigation into pharmaceutical manufacturing standards at a generic drug plant in Eastern Europe
input.document_summary: A 28-page EMA inspection report from a site visit in September 2024 to a pharmaceutical manufacturing plant in Bulgaria. The report identifies 14 'critical findings' related to data integrity, including evidence that quality control test results were altered after initial recording. The plant produces generic versions of three widely prescribed cardiovascular medications distributed across 12 EU member states. The report recommends suspension of the plant's GMP certificate. A handwritten note on page 22 states 'discussed with national authority — grace period agreed.'
